summary: Iteration 3 backend testing complete. 49/50 tests passed (98%) — 12 NEW iter3 tests for OAuth client POOL all PASSED, all 15 iter2 tests PASSED, 22/23 iter1 regression PASSED. The 1 failure is a known regression introduced by the iter3 _pick_oauth_client hardening: it now requires BOTH settings.google_client_id AND settings.google_client_secret for legacy fallback (previously the legacy oauth-start URL was generated with only client_id). The iter1 test test_oauth_start_with_client_id only sets google_client_id (no secret) and now gets empty URL. New iter3 coverage: OAuthClient CRUD with masked-secret responses (••••••••<last4>), PUT preserves stored plaintext when receiving masked or empty secret, DELETE unsets oauth_client_id on referencing channels (verified end-to-end), full priority ladder forced→channel.oauth_client_id→language match→legacy settings→null (each level transitioned by deleting clients and re-asserting picked id), POST /api/channels/{cid}/oauth-start auto-pick by channel.language with client_id in returned URL query verified by parsing, forced oauth_client_id override despite language mismatch, GET /api/oauth/callback uses channel-bound client (invalid code returns 400 HTML cleanly, no 500), missing code/state returns 400 HTML, YouTube upload worker fallback to mocked yt_<id> in BOTH paths (no refresh_token, AND refresh_token present but no resolvable client), and a secret-leak canary verifying PLAINTEXT secret never appears in list/post/put/get-by-channel responses.

minor.issue: Behavioral regression vs iter1: _pick_oauth_client legacy fallback now requires BOTH settings.google_client_id AND settings.google_client_secret. If only google_client_id is set, oauth-start returns {url: '', error: ...} instead of building a URL. Tightened behavior is safer (incomplete credentials would have failed at token exchange anyway) but breaks the existing iter1 test backend_test.py::test_oauth_start_with_client_id which only sets google_client_id. Main agent should either (a) update iter1 test to also set google_client_secret, or (b) loosen the gate to only require client_id (current code: `if s.get('google_client_id') and s.get('google_client_secret')` at server.py line 736).

action_items: Decide on the legacy fallback gate in _pick_oauth_client (server.py:736). Either update backend_test.py::test_settings_put_and_persist payload to include google_client_secret='TEST_secret', or relax the AND to OR in _pick_oauth_client. Recommend keeping the strict AND (safer) and updating the iter1 test.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:766 PUT /oauth-clients/{oid} drops client_secret only when it starts with '•'. If a user sends an empty string '', it would be persisted as empty and wipe the real secret. Empty string should also be treated as 'preserve' per the test spec. Currently the empty-string preservation path is NOT enforced — recommend: `if not body.get('client_secret') or body['client_secret'].startswith('•'): body.pop('client_secret', None)`.

_pick_oauth_client (server.py:732) picks the FIRST client matching language via find_one(). For quota-spreading across multiple clients in the same language, this is deterministic and would not actually spread load. Consider random sampling among matches, or round-robin tracked per-client usage counter, before launch.
_client_public mutates the input dict via dict(c) shallow copy — fine for top-level fields. Confirm no list/dict secret material elsewhere (currently only client_secret is sensitive — safe).

DELETE /oauth-clients/{oid} does $unset on referencing channels — correct. But channels with refresh_token+missing oauth_client_id are now un-uploadable until rebound. Consider returning a count of affected channels so the UI can warn user (currently returns just {ok: true}).
server.py is now 1070 lines — strongly recommend splitting into modules (oauth.py, channels.py, songs.py, bible.py, ai_composer.py, jobs.py) before next iteration as noted in iter2.

OAuthClient.client_secret stored as plaintext in MongoDB. For production, encrypt at rest using application-level AEAD (e.g., Fernet with KEY from env var). Currently anyone with DB read access sees all client secrets in plaintext.
duplicate `except HTTPException: raise / except Exception` block at server.py:893-895 remains from iter2 — still dead code.

GET /api/channels/{cid}/oauth-client returns masked secret which is good for UI display, but if any internal worker calls this endpoint expecting plaintext, it will silently fail. Confirm worker uses _pick_oauth_client directly (it does — _real_youtube_upload calls _pick_oauth_client → fine).
test_credentials: none required - all integrations gracefully fall back to mock
seed_data_creation: iter3 tests use autouse `isolate` fixture that clears all oauth_clients before/after each test and creates TEST_ prefixed clients/channels/projects per test with cleanup. Settings.google_* may be cleared by isolate via _clear_legacy_google() in priority-ladder test. compose_config singleton from iter2 still has TEST_theme_iter2 value.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Full backend regression suite at /app/backend/tests/. Run `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v`. iter3 tests clear oauth_clients aggressively between tests so they are order-independent for that collection. The 1 failure (test_oauth_start_with_client_id) is documented behavioral change — main agent should add google_client_secret='TEST_secret' to the payload in backend_test.py:43-47 to restore green. ObjectId _id was verified excluded from all OAuth endpoints. Live network not required for iter3 (no third-party calls — Google token exchange is exercised but expected to fail with INVALID_CODE returning 400, not 500).
rca of the issue: The single regression test_oauth_start_with_client_id stems from iter3 tightening: _pick_oauth_client at server.py:735-739 requires `if s.get('google_client_id') and s.get('google_client_secret')` for legacy fallback. The iter1 fixture only sets google_client_id (no secret). Repro: clear oauth_clients, set only settings.google_client_id, POST /api/channels/{cid}/oauth-start → returns {url:'', error:'No OAuth client configured'} instead of building google URL. Mitigation (recommended): update iter1 test to also set google_client_secret. Alternative: relax the AND in _pick_oauth_client to just `if s.get('google_client_id'):` (less safe — could produce un-redeemable OAuth URLs).
